expectations.text: Response contains required substrings (PositionIntervalMap, convert_epoch_interval_name_to_position_interval_name, interval_list_name, PositionSource, RawPosition, IntervalList).
expectations.evidence: All required substrings present throughout response.md.

expectations.text: Notes that DLCPoseEstimation's make() already calls convert_epoch... with populate_missing=True
expectations.evidence: 'Note that DLCPoseEstimation's make() already calls convert_epoch_interval_name_to_position_interval_name(..., populate_missing=True) under the hood, so the once-common advice ... doesn't help here'.

expectations.text: Restricts PositionIntervalMap by nwb_file_name + interval_list_name, NOT by epoch
expectations.evidence: 'Restrict by the table's actual primary key — nwb_file_name and interval_list_name, which it inherits from IntervalList. The key is not epoch.'

expectations.text: Walks at least three diagnostic layers
expectations.evidence: Three numbered diagnostic layers: 1 PositionIntervalMap inspection, 2 PositionSource/RawPosition/IntervalList check, 3 TaskEpoch ↔ IntervalList alignment.

expectations.text: Frames PositionSource / RawPosition as prerequisites, NOT as tables PositionIntervalMap queries directly
expectations.evidence: 'PositionIntervalMap's make() doesn't query RawPosition directly. It walks IntervalList rows whose names are recognized as position-interval names ... check the upstreams as prerequisites — they're what put the recognizable interval rows on IntervalList during ingest'.

expectations.text: Treats empty/null PositionIntervalMap as a data-shape problem, not a populate_missing problem
expectations.evidence: 'In the first two cases, the data shape is the problem; you can't fix it by re-running DLCPosV1.populate' and converter-rerun advice 'doesn't help here — that code path has already run'.

expectations.text: Does not tell user to re-run DLCPosV1.populate without first confirming upstream gap
expectations.evidence: Section 'Why just re-run DLCPosV1.populate is the wrong move': 'IndexError isn't transient ... Fix the upstream first ... then retry'.
